what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG icon showing a document outline with a folded corner and the text label PDF inside it.
observations: Uses an SVG viewBox of 0 0 24 24 with explicit width and height of 24.
Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor for the document outline.
Uses stroke width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
Draws the page shape and folded corner with one path element.
Adds a text element reading PDF at x 8 and y 17.
The text uses DM Sans fallback sans-serif, font size 5, font weight 700, no stroke, and fill currentColor.
standouts: Combines a stroked document icon with a filled text label inside the same SVG.
conventions: Uses currentColor so the icon inherits color from its context.
Uses inline SVG geometry rather than external assets or symbol references.
Mixes path-based icon drawing with live SVG text.
concerns: The SVG has no title, description, role, or aria attributes for standalone accessibility.
The text rendering depends on font availability and SVG text behavior.
notes: A compact PDF file icon made from a document outline plus an embedded PDF text label.
questions: Should the live SVG text remain text, or should it be converted to paths for consistent rendering?
Is the icon expected to provide its own accessible name, or is that handled by the caller?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename suggests a PDF file icon, and the SVG renders a document outline labeled PDF.
intention_signals: It appears intended to provide a reusable, color-inheriting PDF document icon for UI use.
novelty: The file-type label is rendered as live SVG text rather than path outlines.
contribution: Contributes a themeable PDF document icon that preserves a clear file outline, folded-corner cue, and embedded file-type label.
summary_for_embedding: A 24px SVG PDF file icon with a currentColor stroked document outline, folded corner, and bold DM Sans PDF text label.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing PDF files in a UI using a compact, color-inheriting SVG asset.
